title: Electronic Flight Bag (EFB) Class 2/3 security posture monitoring - host integrity, performance-data integrity, application allowlist, and MEL-class compliance
exclusions: Excludes Class 1 EFBs that are personally owned and used as electronic-document readers only (no performance-calculation use). Excludes simulator-only EFB units used in training. Excludes flight schools whose EFBs are not connected to operator's flight-planning service.
evidence: Saved search `UC-22.60.7` archived hourly to `audit_evidence` with `do326a_clause="DO-355A-s2-1"`. Every row pins (efb_id, tail_number, last_seen, host_integrity_status, application_allowlist_violations, performance_data_integrity_status, mel_class, severity, risk_score).

compliance.assurance_rationale: DO-355A §2.1 explicitly names the EFB as an asset that the operator must monitor for security posture as part of continuing-airworthiness security. This UC implements the per-EFB integrity + allowlist + performance-data-integrity monitoring.
compliance.controlObjective: Demonstrates that every EFB in the operator's fleet is on its current configuration baseline, its application allowlist is enforced, the performance-data signatures verify against the source-of-truth, and EFB integrity meets the MEL-class limitation.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search archived to `audit_evidence`, the `EFB Security Posture` dashboard, the EFB host events, the operator's EFB Administration policy.

compliance.assurance_rationale: EASA Part-IS IS.OR.205 requires the operator's risk assessment to account for EFB compromise affecting flight safety (incorrect takeoff performance, incorrect runway selection, incorrect emergency procedures).
compliance.controlObjective: Evidences continuous risk-monitoring of the EFB fleet.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Sideload an unsigned third-party application to an EFB iPad outside the operator's MDM allowlist. UC-22.60.7 MUST flag within 10 minutes with risk_score >= 85 and the MDM MUST quarantine the EFB until cleared.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Receive an MDM-pushed application update from the approved app catalogue, with a valid TC-holder / operator signature on the performance-data payload. UC-22.60.7 MUST record `host_integrity_status=clean` and `risk_score=0`.

dataSources: EFB host event logs (Panasonic eX, Boeing OPC, Airbus FlySmart, Lufthansa Systems Lido/Flight, NAVBLUE - sourcetype `efb:audit`), Microsoft Intune MDM events for tablet-class EFB (`intune:device:event`), Jamf Pro events for iPad EFB (`jamf:device:event`), VMware Workspace ONE events (`vmware:airwatch`), EFB performance-data signature verification logs (`efb:perf:signature`), operator's MEL-class register (KV Store `do355a_efb_inventory_lookup`).
app: Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (Splunkbase 742), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (Splunkbase 1621).
spl:
| inputlookup do355a_efb_inventory_lookup
| join type=outer efb_id [
    search index=efb sourcetype="efb:audit" earliest=-24h@h
    | stats latest(_time) AS last_seen latest(host_integrity) AS host_integrity_status latest(application_allowlist_breach_count) AS application_allowlist_violations latest(jailbreak_detected) AS jailbreak_detected by host AS efb_id ]
| join type=outer efb_id [
    search index=efb sourcetype="efb:perf:signature" earliest=-24h@h
    | stats latest(signature_verification_result) AS performance_data_integrity_status latest(perf_data_age_hours) AS perf_data_age_hours by host AS efb_id ]
| fillnull host_integrity_status value="unknown"
| fillnull application_allowlist_violations value=0
| fillnull jailbreak_detected value="false"
| fillnull performance_data_integrity_status value="unknown"
| eval risk_score = case(
    jailbreak_detected = "true", 100,
    host_integrity_status = "compromised", 95,
    application_allowlist_violations > 0, 85,
    performance_data_integrity_status = "fail", 90,
    performance_data_integrity_status = "unknown" AND mel_class = "B", 75,
    host_integrity_status = "unknown" AND isnotnull(last_seen) AND last_seen < relative_time(now(), "-7d"), 60,
    1=1, 0)
| where risk_score > 0
| eval severity = case(risk_score >= 90, "critical", risk_score >= 70, "high", 1=1, "medium")
| sort 0 -risk_score
| table efb_id tail_number last_seen host_integrity_status application_allowlist_violations jailbreak_detected performance_data_integrity_status mel_class severity risk_score
description: Reconciles every EFB in the operator's fleet (whether tail-installed Class 3 or pilot-carried Class 2 iPad / Surface Pro) against MDM events, application allowlist enforcement, host integrity (jailbreak / root) detection, and performance-data signature verification. Surfaces every EFB that is at risk of producing incorrect takeoff / landing performance numbers or that exhibits compromise indicators.
value: An EFB that delivers incorrect takeoff performance numbers (V1, VR, V2, takeoff distance, climb gradient) can cause a runway overrun, a tail strike, or a controlled-flight-into-terrain on departure - all of which are catastrophic safety events. The 2017 Air Canada AC759 incident at SFO and multiple ASRS reports highlight the EFB performance-data integrity threat. This UC closes the gap before the pilot uses bad data.
implementation: (1) Populate `do355a_efb_inventory_lookup` from the operator's EFB administration system (typically a managed Apple Business Manager / Microsoft Intune / VMware Workspace ONE deployment). (2) Configure the EFB application (Boeing OPC, Airbus FlySmart, Lufthansa Lido/Flight, NAVBLUE, AirNav Pro) to emit host integrity and signature verification logs via syslog or HEC to Splunk. (3) Set the operator's MDM to enforce the application allowlist and to push baseline updates. (4) Schedule UC-22.60.7 every 30 minutes; route `risk_score >= 90` at severity P1, `risk_score 70-89` at severity P2. (5) Build the `EFB Security Posture` dashboard.
visualization: (Panel 1) EFB risk-score histogram. (Panel 2) Application-allowlist violations by app. (Panel 3) Performance-data integrity (pass/fail/stale heat map). (Panel 4) MEL-class compliance (Class A / B / C breakdown).

references.title: FAA AC 120-76D - Authorization for Use of Electronic Flight Bags
EASA AMC 20-25A - Airworthiness and operational consideration for Electronic Flight Bags
RTCA DO-355A - Information Security Guidance for Continuing Airworthiness

knownFalsePositives:
1. **Pilot-owned iPad becoming an EFB**: a pilot's personally-owned iPad may briefly appear without MDM enrolment; ensure operator policy requires MDM enrolment before EFB use. 2. **Performance-data age normal**: certain operators use a slower-cadence performance-data refresh (weekly rather than daily); set `mel_class=A` thresholds accordingly. 3. **Test-bench EFB units**: simulator / training EFB units may appear without host integrity reporting; tag with `efb_purpose=training` and exclude.

**Suppression mechanism**: KV Store `do326a_22_60_7_exceptions` with the Director of Flight Operations + CISO documented approval, an explicit expiry date, and a written justification.
reviewer: Aviation Cybersecurity SME Panel + Director of Flight Operations + Chief Pilot + CISO
detailedImplementation:
## Prerequisites

- UC-22.60.1 in production.
- KV Store `do355a_efb_inventory_lookup`.
- EFB application configured to emit `efb:audit` events.
- MDM (Intune, Jamf Pro, Workspace ONE) configured to enforce the application allowlist.

## Step 1 - EFB inventory KV Store

```ini
[do355a_efb_inventory_lookup]
enforceTypes = true
field.efb_id = string
field.tail_number = string
field.efb_class = string
field.mel_class = string
field.efb_app = string
field.last_baseline_version = string
field.in_service = string
```

## Step 2 - Schedule

```ini
[UC-22.60.7: EFB Security Posture]
cron_schedule = */30 * * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -1h@h
dispatch.latest_time = now
actions = summary_index, notable
action.summary_index._name = audit_evidence
action.summary_index.marker = uc=22.60.7,reg=DO-326A,clause=DO-355A-s2-1
alert.severity = 5
```

## Step 3 - Runbooks

**`risk_score >= 90`** (owner: Director of Flight Operations + CISO, severity P1): Within 30 minutes, the affected EFB is removed from service (MDM enforces quarantine); the pilot is issued a known-good standby EFB; the flight is delayed or re-planned with paper performance if MEL allows.

**`risk_score 70-89`** (owner: Director of EFB Administration, severity P2): Within 4 hours, the EFB is re-baselined and re-issued; any flights flown with the affected EFB in the previous 24 hours have their performance numbers re-verified.

## Step 4 - Aviation-specific notes

- **Class 3 EFB (tail-installed)**: behaves more like a Class 1 LSAP than a tablet; UC-22.60.4 also applies to Class 3 EFB software loads.
- **Performance-data feed**: the upstream performance-data service (Boeing OPC, Airbus FlySmart) is also a contracting-activity under IS.OR.235; ensure UC-22.60.5 tracks the supplier-side risk.
- **MEL-class B/C**: Class B and Class C EFB usage permits broader operational use; align the risk threshold accordingly.
grandmaExplanation: Pilots use a tablet called an Electronic Flight Bag to calculate things like how much runway they need for takeoff. We make sure those tablets only run trusted apps and always show the right numbers - because a wrong number on takeoff can cause a serious accident.
